verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Rey Cortiço Mid-morning — 11:00
verdict.reason: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.

conversationalSummary.text: TODAY’s mid-morning around 11:00 at Rey Cortiço is the best bet, but it’s definitely a check-first window—Surfline consistency is a bit hit-and-miss and the wind angle isn’t ideal for that spot. I’d scan the Surfline cams and MEO Beachcam before you go, and only commit if it looks noticeably cleaner than expected. Tide is dropping, so you may still catch the sandbanks at their best for a bit.

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 36 kJ, 11 s period, lully 10/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 10-13s at low tide can still work well on Belgas, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.
best.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for mid-morning — 11:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot

ranked.explanation: Almagreira looks like a small north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 26 kJ, 10 s period, lully 7/100, and wind is the main risk.  An exposed north-coast beach break with more raw swell than Baleal; this is best for small, clean north-coast days when protected spots look underpowered; 10-13s at mid tide works well on exposed beaches, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; wind angle is poor for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Lagide looks like a small window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.3-0.6 m, 35 kJ, 11 s period, lully 14/100, and wind is the main risk.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; 10-13s at low tide is a bit sucky and technical on the reef shelves; SwellMap rating 1/10 Poor — caution.

ranked.explanation: Baleal looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, 18 kJ, 10 s period, lully 9/100, and wind is the main risk.  A broad, forgiving beach setup with several peaks; this is best for spreading out and finding a manageable wave when the bay has enough push; 10-13s at mid tide suits the Baleal sandbanks and gives workable peaks.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; wind angle is poor for this spot; Baleal looks below your bodyboard fun size; Baleal may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Baleal is worth a quick check for afternoon — 16:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
